input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

---

## 1. Determine Required Versions

```bash
# Check your .NET SDK version — this dictates workload versions
dotnet --version

# See what your project targets
grep -E "TargetFramework|MauiVersion" **/*.csproj
```

The `net8.0-android` or `net9.0-android` TFM tells you which Android workload version you need.

---

## 2. Install the MAUI/Android Workload

```bash
# Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK workload as a dependency)
sudo dotnet workload install maui-android

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

---

## 3. Install Java (JDK 17)

Android build tools require JDK 11+. JDK 17 is the current standard:

```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

---

## 4. Install the Android SDK

The .NET Android workload bundles manifest info, but you still need the SDK command-line tools:

```bash
# Find the exact Android API level your project needs
grep "TargetFrameworkVersion\|AndroidSdkVersion\|uses-sdk" **/*.csproj **/AndroidManifest.xml

# Download command-line tools
mkdir -p ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
curl -o cmdline-tools.zip https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip
unzip cmdline-tools.zip -d ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
mv ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest

export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"

# Accept licenses (required for CI)
yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install required components (adjust API level to match your TFM)
# For net8.0-android → API 34; net9.0-android → API 35
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

---

## 5. Point .NET at the Android SDK

```bash
# Either via environment variable:
export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk

# Or via MSBuild property in Directory.Build.props or on the command line:
dotnet build -p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$HOME/android-sdk
```

---

## 6. Build

```bash
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release
```

---

## Finding Correct Versions — Cheat Sheet

| Component | How to find the right version |
|-----------|-------------------------------|
| Android API level | Match your TFM: `net8.0-android` → API 34, `net9.0-android` → API 35 |
| Build-tools | Use same major version as API level (e.g. `34.0.0`) |
| JDK | 17 is safe for all current MAUI versions |
| Workload | `dotnet workload search maui` shows available versions |
| Cmdline-tools | Always use `_latest.zip` from Google |

---

## Quick Validation Checklist

```bash
dotnet workload list          # maui-android should appear
java -version                 # 17.x
sdkmanager --list | head -20  # shows installed SDK components
dotnet build --no-restore -f net8.0-android  # dry-run build
```

If your project uses specific NuGet packages that pin Android SDK versions, check `obj/` after restore for `AndroidApiInfo.targets` — it will show exactly which API/build-tools version MSBuild expects.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a headless Linux CI environment to build a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK installed but need everything else. Let me provide a comprehensive plan based on my knowledge of MAUI Android build requirements.
